concept: Laundry / Cloth Gathered Through the Washing Cycle
description: Garments and household cloth gathered for washing or moving through drying and folding afterward. The noun refers to one load or to the material waiting at a particular stage. 'wethalu' (garment) names something worn; 'wethate' gathers the cloth by the domestic work currently being done to it.
sound_symbolism: Rounded 'we' glides into stressed dental 'tha' and broad 'a'. The opening gives 'wetha' (cloth) whole. Final 'te' returns the tongue to the teeth for a clean stop and balanced 'e'. The same place of contact frames the open stressed center in breath first, closure second.
grammatical_notes: A mass or countable noun according to context: 'mia ha wethate sawa.' (I wash this laundry.) Work terms such as 'riporu' (task), 'rilowa' (assign), or 'ritako' (workload) may describe its labor without changing the household noun.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Calling the gathered cloth laundry puts the repeated work where people can share, assign, refuse, or appreciate it.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Washing day followed water, weather, fuel, and the time needed for cloth to dry before night.

semantic_domains.activity: cloth moving through washing, drying, or folding
semantic_domains.community: a recurring domestic workload within a household
semantic_domains.physical: garments and household cloth gathered as one load
